judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are strong and nearly identical on (a)-(c), but Report 1 edges ahead on (d) by recommending dropping the hardcoded webhook count rather than merely updating it to 5.

report_1.evidence: New refund calls should live in `medibill/backend/app/billing/gateway.py:1-18` ... A refund feature should add a `refund(...)` accessor there ... `medibill/backend/app/api/invoices.py:4,14` ... builds its own `chargekit.Client(...)` ... `medibill/backend/app/workers/dunning.py:2,9` — same pattern ... The only documented exception is `medibill/backend/app/billing/errors.py:6`, which imports just `chargekit.errors`

report_2.evidence: a new `refund()` call should be added as a function in `gateway.py` ... `medibill/backend/app/api/invoices.py:4,14` ... calls `chargekit.Client(...)` directly ... `medibill/backend/app/workers/dunning.py:2,9` ... does the same ... `app/billing/errors.py` as the sole, explicit exception (importing only error classes)

report_1.evidence: `InvoiceOut` in `medibill/backend/app/models/invoice.py:16-22`, has six fields: `id, tenant_id, amount_cents, currency, status, external_ref`. The extra `external_ref` field ... added per migration `medibill/backend/migrations/0006_add_invoice_external_ref.sql` ... is absent from the documented response shape

report_2.evidence: The actual `InvoiceOut` dataclass in `medibill/backend/app/models/invoice.py:16-22` has six fields: `id, tenant_id, amount_cents, currency, status, external_ref`. `external_ref` is populated by `charge_invoice` (`invoices.py:17`) and serialized by `InvoiceOut.from_model` ... The doc is missing this field entirely

report_1.evidence: this doc should be corrected now, as part of the same change that's being reviewed/merged — not deferred to the refund work ... whichever PR fixes the code (or formally exempts it) must update `ARCHITECTURE.md` in that same commit ... not as a follow-up

report_2.evidence: It should be corrected before any refund work lands, as part of this same change — not after ... Doing this now (rather than deferring) matters because a new refund feature is exactly the kind of change that gets built by copy-pasting an existing call site

report_1.evidence: the count "4" is wrong (should be 5, or the count should be dropped per project documentation's "don't cite a number: read the source" guidance, since this is exactly the kind of number that silently drifts as handlers are added)
